name: application-gateway
title: Azure Load Balancer — Application Gateway v2
summary: Creates an Application Gateway (Standard_v2 or WAF_v2) with autoscaling, zone deployment, Key Vault-sourced SSL certificates via managed identity, custom probes, HTTP(S) listeners, and basic routing rules.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: resource_group_name
module.description: Name of the existing resource group for the application gateway.

module.name: location
module.description: Azure region for the application gateway.

module.name: gateway_subnet_id
module.description: ID of the dedicated subnet for the application gateway (recommended /24, no other workloads). Supplied by ID — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: sku_name
module.description: v2 SKU name: Standard_v2 or WAF_v2.

module.name: waf_policy_id
module.description: ID of an existing WAF policy to attach (WAF_v2 SKU). Null = no policy.

module.name: backend_pools
module.description: Backend address pools keyed by logical name (FQDNs and/or IPs; empty pool = NIC-joined by the consumer).

module.name: backend_http_settings
module.type: map(object({ port = number protocol = optional(string, "Http") cookie_based_affinity = optional(string, "Disabled") request_timeout = optional(number, 30) pick_host_name_from_backend_address = optional(bool, false) probe_key = optional(string) }))
module.description: Backend HTTP settings keyed by logical name.

module.name: probes
module.type: map(object({ protocol = optional(string, "Http") path = string interval = optional(number, 30) timeout = optional(number, 30) unhealthy_threshold = optional(number, 3) pick_host_name_from_backend_http_settings = optional(bool, true) match_status_codes = optional(list(string), ["200-399"]) }))
module.description: Custom health probes keyed by logical name.

module.name: http_listeners
module.type: map(object({ frontend_port = number protocol = optional(string, "Http") host_name = optional(string) ssl_certificate_key = optional(string) }))
module.description: HTTP(S) listeners keyed by logical name. HTTPS listeners reference an SSL certificate by logical key.

module.name: ssl_certificates
module.description: SSL certificates keyed by logical name, sourced from Key Vault. The gateway's managed identity (identity_ids) needs Key Vault secret-read access.

module.name: identity_ids
module.description: User-assigned managed identity IDs for the gateway (required for Key Vault certificate access).

module.name: request_routing_rules
module.type: map(object({ priority = number listener_key = string backend_pool_key = string backend_http_settings_key = string }))
module.description: Basic routing rules keyed by logical name, joining listener -> pool + settings by logical keys.

outputs.name: lb_address
outputs.description: Public IP address of the application gateway frontend.

outputs.name: listener_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical listener name to inner listener name (Application Gateway inner blocks have no standalone IDs — names are the stable handle).

outputs.name: backend_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical backend pool name to inner pool name. Use with azurerm_network_interface_application_gateway_backend_address_pool_association in the consumer root.

outputs.name: backend_address_pool_resource_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical backend pool name to the full Azure resource ID of the pool (needed for NIC associations).
